responses: Hello! I'm AgriBot 🌱 Ask me about crop diseases, soil, irrigation, or upload a leaf image!
Hi there! Ready to help with your farming questions. What would you like to know?
Happy farming! Come back anytime 🌾
Goodbye! Wishing you a healthy harvest!
You're welcome! Feel free to ask more anytime.
Happy to help! Upload a leaf image if you spot any symptoms.

I'm AgriBot 🌱 Here's what I can do:
1. 📸 Detect crop diseases from leaf photos (CNN AI)
2. 💬 Answer questions on crops: soil, irrigation, fertilizer, pests
3. 🎙️ Understand voice questions
4. 💊 Suggest specific disease remedies

Try uploading a leaf image or asking 'How to treat tomato blight?'

🍎 Apple Scab (Venturia inaequalis):
• Apply myclobutanil or captan fungicide at green tip stage
• Rake and remove all fallen leaves
• Prune for air circulation
• Apply lime sulfur spray during dormancy
• Use resistant varieties like Liberty or Freedom

🍎 Apple Black Rot:
• Prune and destroy all infected wood and fruit
• Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl
• Avoid wounding trees during pruning
• Maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization

🍎 Apple Cedar-Apple Rust:
• Remove nearby juniper/cedar trees if possible
• Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb from pink stage through 3rd cover spray
• Use resistant apple varieties
• Spray every 7-10 days during wet spring weather

🌽 Corn Common Rust (Puccinia sorghi):
• Apply propiconazole or azoxystrobin at first sign
• Plant resistant hybrids rated for rust resistance
• Scout fields weekly during vegetative stage
• Rust develops fast in cool, moist conditions — act quickly

🌽 Corn Leaf Blight:
• Apply fungicide (propiconazole) at tasseling stage
• Use resistant hybrids — check seed catalog for NCLB rating
• Rotate crops — residue on soil surface is the main source
• Avoid dense plant populations; maintain good airflow

🍇 Grape Black Rot (Guignardia bidwellii):
• Remove all mummified berries and infected canes
• Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil from budbreak through fruit set
• Ensure good canopy airflow through pruning
• Critical spray window: 2-4 weeks after bloom

🍇 Grape Esca (Black Measles):
• No effective chemical cure — manage preventively
• Avoid large pruning wounds; apply wound sealant
• Remove and destroy severely infected vines
• Keep vines vigorous with balanced nutrition
• Avoid water stress — drip irrigate regularly

🍇 Grape Leaf Blight (Isariopsis):
• Apply copper-based fungicide or mancozeb
• Remove infected leaves and destroy debris
• Improve air circulation by proper canopy management
• Spray preventively during humid weather

🥔 Potato Early Blight (Alternaria solani):
• Apply mancozeb (2g/L) or chlorothalonil at first sign
• Remove and destroy infected leaves
• Ensure adequate nitrogen — deficiency worsens disease
• Avoid excessive irrigation
• Rotate crops — do not plant potatoes in same field every year

🥔 Potato Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans):
• Apply Ridomil Gold or Dithane M-45 (2.5g/L) every 7 days
• Destroy all infected tubers — do NOT compost them
• Ensure good field drainage
• Harvest before disease spreads to tubers
• Use certified disease-free seed potatoes next season

🍅 Tomato Bacterial Spot (Xanthomonas):
• Use copper-based bactericide (2g/L) every 10 days
• Remove infected leaves and plant debris
• Avoid working in wet fields — spreads by water splash
• Use certified disease-free seeds
• Rotate crops for 2-3 years

🍅 Tomato Early Blight (Alternaria solani):
• Remove infected leaves immediately
• Apply copper-based fungicide or mancozeb (2g/L) every 7-10 days
• Avoid overhead irrigation — water at base only
• Rotate crops yearly
• Use resistant varieties like Iron Lady or Defiant

🍅 Tomato Late Blight (Phytophthora infestans):
• Apply fungicides with chlorothalonil or metalaxyl immediately
• Remove and destroy ALL infected plant parts
• Avoid wetting foliage — use drip irrigation
• This spreads rapidly in cool wet conditions — act within 24 hours

🍅 Tomato Leaf Mold (Passalora fulva):
• Increase ventilation — common in greenhouses
• Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil
• Remove lower affected leaves
• Reduce humidity below 85%
• Space plants wider to improve airflow

🍅 Tomato Septoria Leaf Spot:
• Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb at first sign
• Remove infected lower leaves
• Avoid overhead watering
• Mulch around base to prevent soil splash
• Rotate crops — disease survives in soil and debris

🍅 Tomato Spider Mites:
• Spray with water forcefully to dislodge mites
• Apply insecticidal soap or neem oil (5ml/L)
• Introduce predatory mites (Phytoseiulus persimilis) for biological control
• Avoid dusty conditions — mites thrive in dust
• Abamectin or bifenazate for severe infestations

🍅 Tomato Target Spot (Corynespora):
• Apply azoxystrobin or mancozeb fungicide
• Remove infected plant material
• Improve air circulation
• Avoid prolonged leaf wetness
• Use mulch to prevent soil splash

🍅 Tomato Mosaic Virus:
• No chemical cure — remove and destroy infected plants immediately
• Wash hands and tools before touching healthy plants (virus spreads by contact)
• Control aphids — they transmit the virus
• Use certified virus-free seeds and transplants
• Plant resistant varieties like Shady Lady

🍅 Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus (TYLCV):
• Spread by whitefly — control whitefly urgently with imidacloprid 0.3ml/L
• Use yellow sticky traps to monitor whitefly
• Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
• Use virus-resistant varieties: TY1, Shanty, Striker
• Use insect-proof net houses in severe infestation areas

🍅 Tomato Crop Profile:
• Soil: Well-drained loamy, pH 6.0-7.0
• Temperature: 20-27°C (day), 15-20°C (night)
• Rainfall: 600-1200mm/year; avoid waterlogging
• Season: Kharif (Jun-Jul), Rabi (Oct-Nov)
• Irrigation: Drip preferred, every 3-5 days
• Fertilizer: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha + FYM 25 t/ha
• Yield: 25-35 t/ha (hybrid varieties)

🌾 Rice Crop Profile:
• Soil: Clay loam or silty clay, pH 5.5-6.5
• Temperature: 25-35°C optimal
• Rainfall: 1000-2000mm
• Season: Kharif (Jun-Nov), Rabi (Nov-Apr) South India
• Irrigation: 5cm standing water during vegetative stage
• Fertilizer: NPK 120:60:60 kg/ha, split application
• Yield: 4-6 t/ha (HYV), 6-8 t/ha (hybrid)

🌾 Wheat Crop Profile:
• Soil: Loam to clay loam, pH 6.0-7.5
• Temperature: 15-25°C; cooler at maturity
• Rainfall: 450-650mm
• Season: Rabi — sow Oct-Dec, harvest Mar-May
• Irrigation: 4-5 critical irrigations
• Fertilizer: NPK 120:60:40 kg/ha
• Yield: 3-5 t/ha

🥔 Potato Crop Profile:
• Soil: Sandy loam, pH 5.2-6.4; avoid waterlogging
• Temperature: 15-25°C; tubers form best below 20°C
• Rainfall: 500-700mm; avoid excess moisture
• Season: Rabi (Oct-Jan) North India; year-round in hills
• Irrigation: Every 7-10 days; stop 2 weeks before harvest
• Fertilizer: NPK 180:90:90 kg/ha
• Yield: 25-35 t/ha

🌽 Maize Crop Profile:
• Soil: Sandy loam to loamy, pH 5.8-7.0
• Temperature: 21-30°C optimal
• Rainfall: 500-800mm
• Season: Kharif (Jun-Oct), Rabi (Oct-Feb)
• Fertilizer: NPK 180:80:60 kg/ha; nitrogen in 3 splits
• Yield: 6-10 t/ha (hybrid)

🧪 Fertilizer Guide:
• Pre-planting: Apply FYM/compost (10-25 t/ha) + basal NPK
• Vegetative stage: Top-dress with nitrogen (urea)
• Flowering: Potassium (MOP) boosts fruit quality
• Nitrogen deficiency: Yellowing of older leaves — apply urea 2g/L
• Organic options: Vermicompost, neem cake, bone meal
• Get a free Soil Health Card from Krishi Kendra for accurate doses

💧 Irrigation Guide:
• Drip: Most efficient — 40-60% water saving (ideal for vegetables/fruits)
• Furrow: Good for row crops like maize, sugarcane
• Sprinkler: Good for wheat; avoid for fungal-prone crops
• Rule: Water when top 3-5cm soil feels dry
• Critical stages: Germination, flowering, fruit set
• Never water in the evening — promotes fungal disease

🐛 Pest Management:
• Aphids: Imidacloprid 0.3ml/L or neem oil 5ml/L
• Whitefly: Yellow sticky traps + thiamethoxam 0.3g/L
• Thrips: Spinosad 0.5ml/L + reflective mulch
• Caterpillars: Bt spray (Bacillus thuringiensis) — organic & safe
• Mealybug: Dimethoate 1ml/L drench
• IPM rule: Scout → biological → chemical as last resort
• Always spray early morning or evening; wear protective gear

🌍 Soil Health Guide:
• Ideal pH for most crops: 6.0-7.0
• Acidic soil (pH<6): Add lime (CaCO3) 200-400 kg/ha
• Alkaline soil (pH>7.5): Add gypsum or sulfur 200 kg/ha
• Black soil: High water retention — good for cotton, soybean
• Red soil: Low nutrient content — add organic matter
• Sandy soil: Add organic matter; irrigate more frequently
• Free Soil Health Card available at your nearest Krishi Kendra

🌿 Organic Farming:
• Soil building: Vermicompost 5-10 t/ha + green manure (Dhaincha)
• Pest control: Neem oil 5ml/L, Beauveria bassiana, pheromone traps
• Weed: Mulching with straw or black polythene
• Disease: Trichoderma viride (5g/L) as bio-fungicide
• Certification: Apply through APEDA or state organic board
• Premium: Organic produce fetches 20-40% higher market price
